ddls:
CREATE TABLE "posts" (
  "post_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the social media platform where the post is hosted */,
  "post_url" TEXT /* URL of the post */,
  "post_content" TEXT /* Content of the post */,
  "post_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the post was published */,
  "post_author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who published the post */,
  "post_type" TEXT /* Type of post (e.g., video, image, text) */,
  "post_tags" TEXT /* Relevant tags or keywords associated with the post */,
  "post_engagement" REAL /* Overall engagement score of the post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_post_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_platform_id FOREIGN KEY ("platform_id") REFERENCES platforms ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform */,
  "platform_name" TEXT /* Name of the social media platform */,
  "platform_url" TEXT /* Base URL of the platform */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the platform */,
  "platform_api_key" TEXT /* API key for each platform */,
  "platform_quota" INTEGER /* Quota limit for each platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the social media platform where the author is active */,
  "author_profile_url" TEXT /* URL of the author's profile on the social media platform */,
  "author_followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers the author has on the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observations" (
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* Reference to the post being observed */,
  "param_source" TEXT /* Source of the observation parameters */,
  "observing_by" TEXT /* Method used for observation */,
  "time_observed" INTEGER /* Time at which the post was observed */,
  "observed_comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of comments observed */,
  "true_comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of true comments */,
  "simulated_comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of simulated comments */,
  "true_root_comments" INTEGER /* Number of true root comments */,
  "sim_root_comments" INTEGER /* Number of simulated root comments */,
  "true_depth" INTEGER /* Depth of the true comment tree */,
  "true_breadth" INTEGER /* Breadth of the true comment tree */,
  "simulated_depth" INTEGER /* Depth of the simulated comment tree */,
  "simulated_breadth" INTEGER /* Breadth of the simulated comment tree */,
  "true_structural_virality" REAL /* Structural virality of the true comments */,
  "sim_structural_virality" REAL /* Structural virality of the simulated comments */,
  "true_lifetime" INTEGER /* Lifetime of the true comments */,
  "sim_lifetime" INTEGER /* Lifetime of the simulated comments */,
  "mepdl_min" INTEGER /* Minimum value of MEPDL (Minimum Expected Path Depth and Length) */,
  "mepdl_max" INTEGER /* Maximum value of MEPDL */,
  "disconnected" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the observation is disconnected */,
  "connecting_edges" INTEGER /* Number of connecting edges */,
  "observation_status" TEXT /* Status of the observation (e.g., "complete", "in_progress") */,
  "observation_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the observation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("observation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* Reference to the post the comment belongs to */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text content of the comment */,
  "comment_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was posted */,
  "comment_author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the comment */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment, if the comment is a reply */,
  "comment_likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes on the comment */,
  "comment_replies" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_comment_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("comment_author_id") REFERENCES comment_authors ("comment_author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_parent_comment_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment_id") REFERENCES comments ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comment_authors" (
  "comment_author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment author */,
  "comment_author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the comment author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the comment author */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the social media platform where the author is active */,
  "comment_author_profile_url" TEXT /* URL of the comment author's profile on the social media platform */,
  "comment_author_followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers the comment author has on the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin, viewer) */,
  "user_last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "user_subscription_plan" TEXT /* User's subscription plan (e.g., free, premium) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "entity_id" TEXT /* ID of the entity being accessed (post or comment) */,
  "entity_type" TEXT /* Type of the entity being accessed (post or comment) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the entity */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the entity was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used to access the platform (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "access_browser" TEXT /* Browser used to access the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "simulations" (
  "simulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each simulation */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* Reference to the post being simulated */,
  "simulation_type" TEXT /* Type of simulation (e.g., engagement, virality) */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used for the simulation */,
  "simulated_comment_count" INTEGER /* Number of simulated comments */,
  "sim_root_comments" INTEGER /* Number of simulated root comments */,
  "simulated_depth" INTEGER /* Depth of the simulated comment tree */,
  "simulated_breadth" INTEGER /* Breadth of the simulated comment tree */,
  "sim_structural_virality" REAL /* Structural virality of the simulated comments */,
  "sim_lifetime" INTEGER /* Lifetime of the simulated comments */,
  "mepdl_min" INTEGER /* Minimum value of MEPDL (Minimum Expected Path Depth and Length) */,
  "mepdl_max" INTEGER /* Maximum value of MEPDL */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the simulation was created */,
  "simulation_status" TEXT /* Status of the simulation (e.g., "complete", "in_progress") */,
  "simulation_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the simulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("simulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_simulations_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)
